steps.title: Identify the text's organizational pattern
steps.content: The passage uses a specific-to-general structure. It begins by presenting concrete Muscogee words (hatki, hathaki), moves to explaining the linguistic principle they exemplify (reduplication), and concludes by asserting that this principle occurs frequently in the language.

steps.title: Match the pattern to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D tracks the passage's actual progression: it presents specific examples, then explains the general principle they exemplify, then states the principle occurs frequently.

The other choices misidentify the passage's content, introducing translation difficulties, scholarly debate, and cross-linguistic comparisons that the text doesn't address.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
